pi_items.source: Task description (point 5): 'Create a calibration report CSV at /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_report.csv'. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 14 and setup_task.sh line 23.
Task description (point 4): 'Save it to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_120bpm.png'. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 31.

Task description (point 3) explicitly lists three test points: 60 BPM, 90 BPM, 120 BPM. Verified against ProSim 4 Vital Signs Simulator specification which uses 60, 80, 90, 120 as standard ECG rate calibration points. These are industry-standard test values for medical device vital signs calibration (https://www.flukebiomedical.com/sites/default/files/resources/Prosim4_ENG_E_W.PDF).

Task description (point 4): 'While the HR is set to 120 BPM, take a screenshot...'. This matches the final expected_points value and the normal elevated heart rate test point used in medical device validation.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic medical device calibration task using OpenICE simulator. All metadata claims are verified: the calibration report should be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_report.csv, the evidence screenshot to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_120bpm.png, and the task requires testing at three standard medical device test points (60, 90, and 120 BPM), with final verification at 120 BPM. These test points are standard values used in real medical vital signs simulators like ProSim 4. The data is entirely simulated using OpenICE's Multiparameter Monitor simulator.
